page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.hfc7.large is a compute-optimized instance powered by an Intel Xeon 8369HB processor utilizing the x86_64 architecture. It features 2 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 2 physical cores with 1.0 thread per core, running at a clock speed of 3.3 GHz. The CPU includes 32 KB L1d cache, 1.0 MB L2 cache, and 33 MB L3 cache. It is equipped with 4.0 GB of system memory, providing 2.0 GB of RAM per core. This instance does not include local storage or a GPU, and it operates on a KVM hypervisor with a baseline network bandwidth of 1 Gbps.

Performance benchmarks indicate a divergence between single-core and multi-core capabilities. Single-core performance is moderate, with stress-ng single-core CPU and Geekbench single-core scores falling into the average category. Single-threaded tasks like gzip compression and bzip3 decompression also demonstrate average performance. However, multi-core and memory-bound benchmarks are weak, with stress-ng multi-core, Geekbench multi-core, and Passmark CPU scores ranking in the bottom 10%. Memory bandwidth is also limited, with large-block read and write speeds registering at 15.84 GB/sec and 13.29 GB/sec respectively.

The qualitative cost profile of the ecs.hfc7.large reflects its entry-level compute-optimized hardware class, offering dedicated CPU resources without the overhead of bundled local storage. The primary tradeoff is the low memory-to-vCPU ratio and poor multi-core scaling, which limits its utility for memory-intensive or highly parallelized applications. It is best suited for lightweight, single-threaded compute tasks, basic web serving, text processing, or development environments that benefit from dedicated high-frequency CPU cores but do not require large memory footprints or high multi-threaded throughput.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.hfc7.large is a compute-optimized instance featuring 2 dedicated vCPUs and 4.0 GB of RAM, powered by an Intel Xeon 8369HB processor running at 3.3 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. Operating on a KVM hypervisor, this instance provides a baseline network bandwidth of 1 Gbps but lacks local storage and GPU acceleration. Benchmarks show average single-core performance, as seen in single-threaded compression and Geekbench single-core tests, contrasted with poor multi-core and memory bandwidth results. The hardware configuration presents a clear tradeoff, offering dedicated high-frequency CPU cores alongside a limited memory capacity of 2.0 GB per core. This profile makes the ecs.hfc7.large suitable for lightweight, single-threaded compute workloads, basic text processing, and small-scale web serving where multi-threaded performance and high memory capacity are not critical.
og_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.hfc7.large is a compute-optimized instance featuring 2 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 4.0 GB of RAM, and 1 Gbps network bandwidth, designed for lightweight single-threaded workloads.
meta_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.hfc7.large features 2 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 4.0 GB RAM, and 1 Gbps bandwidth for single-threaded compute workloads.
tagline: A dedicated dual core compute instance featuring high frequency Intel processors designed for lightweight and single threaded workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by a 3.3 GHz Intel Xeon 8369HB processor with 2 dedicated physical cores.
Equipped with 4.0 GB of system memory and a baseline network bandwidth of 1 Gbps.
Delivers average single-core performance for single-threaded compression and text processing.
Lacks local storage and GPU acceleration, relying entirely on network-attached storage.
Best suited for lightweight compute tasks, basic web serving, and development environments.
